# WindowedPainting { #tspaint.WindowedPainting }

```python
tspaint.WindowedPainting(
    out_dir,
    window_size,
    seqlen,
    bounds,
    Q,
    pi,
    w,
    queries,
    labels=None,
    default_deadband=DEFAULT_DEADBAND,
)
```

Handle over a windowed, streamed painting written to a directory by
``paint(ts, …, window_size=…, out_dir=…)``.

The heavy per-position posteriors stay on disk — one ``window_NNNNN.npz`` :class:`Painting` per
genomic window, plus a ``manifest.json`` describing the single shared fit — so this object is
lightweight (just the fit and the window index). Iterate windows lazily with :meth:`windows` (one
loaded at a time), or reassemble the whole genome with :meth:`painting` (holds it all).

## Attributes {.doc-section .doc-section-attributes}

<code>[**out_dir**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[str](`str`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Directory holding the ``window_NNNNN.npz`` files and ``manifest.json``.

<code>[**window_size, seqlen**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[float](`float`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Window width and the painted genome's sequence length (bp).

<code>[**bounds**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[list](`list`)\[[tuple](`tuple`)\[[int](`int`), [float](`float`), [float](`float`)\]\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   ``(k, lo, hi)`` per window, in genome order.

<code>[**Q, pi, w, queries, labels**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} []{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   The single shared fit — the same for every window.

<code>[**default_deadband**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[float](`float`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Default dead-band carried into every per-window / reassembled :class:`Painting` (for :meth:`Painting.segments`). Default :data:`~tspaint.output.DEFAULT_DEADBAND` (``0.4``).

## Methods

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [load](#tspaint.WindowedPainting.load) | Reopen a directory previously written by windowed :func:`paint` (reads ``manifest.json``). |
| [painting](#tspaint.WindowedPainting.painting) | Reassemble the full genome-wide :class:`Painting` from the per-window files. |
| [windows](#tspaint.WindowedPainting.windows) | Yield ``(lo, hi, Painting)`` per window, loading one file at a time (bounded memory). |

### load { #tspaint.WindowedPainting.load }

```python
tspaint.WindowedPainting.load(out_dir)
```

Reopen a directory previously written by windowed :func:`paint` (reads ``manifest.json``).

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**out_dir**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[str](`str`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   A directory written by ``paint(..., window_size=…, out_dir=out_dir)`` — it must hold a ``manifest.json`` (the shared fit and window bounds) alongside the ``window_NNNNN.npz`` files.

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[WindowedPainting](`tspaint.api.WindowedPainting`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   A lightweight handle over the per-window files (the window index and shared fit only; the per-window posteriors stay on disk until :meth:`windows` / :meth:`painting`).

### painting { #tspaint.WindowedPainting.painting }

```python
tspaint.WindowedPainting.painting()
```

Reassemble the full genome-wide :class:`Painting` from the per-window files.

Loads every window and concatenates by genomic position — this holds the whole-genome
posteriors in memory (the cost streaming avoided), so call it only when you can afford it,
or after narrowing to a manageable region.

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[Painting](`tspaint.api.Painting`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   The stitched genome-wide painting (``ts=None``; carries the shared fit ``Q``/``π``/``w``, ``queries``, ``labels`` and :attr:`default_deadband`, and tiles ``[0, L)`` exactly as a single-pass paint would).

### windows { #tspaint.WindowedPainting.windows }

```python
tspaint.WindowedPainting.windows()
```

Yield ``(lo, hi, Painting)`` per window, loading one file at a time (bounded memory).

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[Iterator](`Iterator`)\[[tuple](`tuple`)\[[float](`float`), [float](`float`), [Painting](`tspaint.api.Painting`)\]\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   One ``(lo, hi, Painting)`` per window in genome order — the window's ``[lo, hi)`` bounds and its :class:`Painting`, loaded from ``window_NNNNN.npz`` on demand (one held at a time).
